amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: The pentose phosphate pathway (PPP) in E. coli generates NADPH for biosynthesis and ribose-5-phosphate for nucleotides. The oxidative phase (G6P → ribulose-5-P) produces 2 NADPH via Zwf and Gnd. The non-oxidative phase interconverts sugar phosphates via transketolase and transaldolase, connecting to glycolysis. The pathway has multiple branch points and can operate in different modes depending on cellular needs.

graph TD %% Entry Points A[Glucose-6-Phosphate] --> B{OR: Which
Pathway?} B -->|Oxidative PPP| C[Enter Oxidative Phase] B -->|Glycolysis| D[Phosphoglucose Isomerase] B -->|Both| E[Flux Split] %% Oxidative Phase Step 1 C --> F["Zwf: G6P Dehydrogenase"] F --> G{AND: NADP+
Available?} G -->|No| H[\Enzyme Inhibited/] G -->|Yes| I[6-Phosphogluconolactone] I --> J[NADPH #1 Produced] %% Oxidative Phase Step 2 I --> K["Pgl: 6-Phosphogluconolactonase"] K --> L[6-Phosphogluconate] %% Oxidative Phase Step 3 L --> M["Gnd: 6PG Dehydrogenase"] M --> N{AND: NADP+
Available?} N -->|No| O[\Enzyme Inhibited/] N -->|Yes| P[Ribulose-5-Phosphate] P --> Q[NADPH #2 Produced] P --> R[CO2 Released] %% Branch Point P --> S{OR: Cellular
Need?} S -->|NADPH for Biosynthesis| T[End Oxidative - NADPH Output] S -->|Ribose-5-P for Nucleotides| U[Enter Isomerization] S -->|Recycle to Glycolysis| V[Enter Non-oxidative Phase] %% Isomerization U --> W["Rpe: Ru5P Epimerase"] W --> X[Xylulose-5-Phosphate] U --> Y["Rpi: Ru5P Isomerase"] Y --> Z[Ribose-5-Phosphate] %% OR Gate: Nucleotide Synthesis Z --> AA{OR: Which
Nucleotide?} AA -->|Purine| AB[PRPP Synthetase] AA -->|Pyrimidine| AC[UMP Synthesis] AA -->|Histidine| AD[Histidine Biosynthesis] AB --> AE([Nucleotide Biosynthesis]) AC --> AE AD --> AE %% Non-oxidative Phase V --> AF[Mix of Sugar Phosphates] X --> AF Z --> AF %% Transketolase Reaction 1 AF --> AG["TktA: Transketolase"] AG --> AH[/X5P + R5P → S7P + G3P/] AH --> AI[Sedoheptulose-7-P] AH --> AJ[Glyceraldehyde-3-P] %% Transaldolase Reaction AI --> AK["TalB: Transaldolase"] AJ --> AK AK --> AL[S7P + G3P → F6P + E4P] AL --> AM[Fructose-6-Phosphate] AL --> AN[Erythrose-4-Phosphate] %% Transketolase Reaction 2 AN --> AO["/TktB: Transketolase/"] X --> AO AO --> AP[E4P + X5P → F6P + G3P] AP --> AQ[Fructose-6-Phosphate #2] AP --> AR[Glyceraldehyde-3-P #2] %% Return to Glycolysis AM --> AS{OR: Which
Fate?} AQ --> AS AS -->|Lower Glycolysis| AT[Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate] AS -->|Gluconeogenesis| AU[Glucose-6-Phosphate] AJ --> AV{OR: Glycolysis
Fate?} AR --> AV AV -->|Pyruvate| AW[Lower Glycolysis] AV -->|Recycle| AU %% Operational Modes AT --> AX["Mode 1: Max NADPH"] AU --> AY["Mode 2: Max Ribose-5-P"] AU --> AZ["Mode 3: Recycle to Glycolysis"] %% Outputs T --> BA([NADPH for Fatty Acid Synthesis]) J --> BA Q --> BA AW --> BB([Pyruvate to TCA]) AX --> BB AY --> BC([Pentose Sugars]) AZ --> BD([Glycolytic Intermediates]) %% Styling name: Pentose Phosphate Pathway (Oxidative and Non-oxidative)
notes: PPP shows high OR gates for multiple operational modes and branch points.
scientificAccuracy: Based on biochemical characterization of E. coli PPP enzymes.

sources.title: The pentose phosphate pathway in Escherichia coli.
